name: ExtensionCapabilityStatement_AcceptLanguage
title: R5: Languages supported (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `CapabilityStatement.acceptLanguage` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`CapabilityStatement.acceptLanguage` 0..* `code`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `CapabilityStatement.acceptLanguage` 0..* `code`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `CapabilityStatement.acceptLanguage` has a context of CapabilityStatement based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `CapabilityStatement`.
Element `CapabilityStatement.acceptLanguage` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
Element `CapabilityStatement.acceptLanguage` has a context of CapabilityStatement based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `CapabilityStatement`.
Element `CapabilityStatement.acceptLanguage` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
In general, if a server gets a request with an Accept-Language that it doesn't support, it should still reutrn the resource, just in its default language for the resource.

element.definition: A list of the languages supported by this implementation that are usefully supported in the ```Accept-Language``` header.
element.comment: In general, if a server gets a request with an Accept-Language that it doesn't support, it should still reutrn the resource, just in its default language for the resource.
